title: **Balance Sheet Overview – As of December 31, 2026**
content:
The primary driver of growth in total assets was a sharp increase in cash and deposits, which rose by $4.7 billion to reach $81.668 million — the largest absolute dollar change among all asset categories during this period.
Total Assets stood at $10,493,110 thousand (in thousands), reflecting an overall 1.6% increase quarter-over-quarter and a 12.9% year-over-year gain driven largely by liquidity inflows into cash positions.

Cash and deposits expanded significantly to strengthen the institution’s near-term liquidity position as funds were redeployed from other asset classes or received externally, while investment securities grew modestly by $5.9 million (0.3%) over the quarter but declined slightly year-over-year (-1.6%), indicating a partial rotation away from fixed-income holdings toward more liquid assets.
Loans and leases remained stable at $6,628,090 thousand with minimal quarterly movement (+$13M or 0.2% QoQ), suggesting steady lending activity without aggressive expansion in this core earning-asset category during the period.
Other Assets increased by $14.5 million (17.4%) quarter-over-quarter and other investments added a small amount, while accrued dividends payable decreased slightly (-$16K) as interest obligations were settled; these minor shifts collectively contributed less than 2% of total asset value but reflect normal operational timing differences.

The largest balance on the asset side remains Loans and Leases at $6.6 billion (63.2%), confirming its role as the dominant source of earnings potential for Campus Credit Union, while Cash and Deposits now represent nearly 8% — up from roughly 5–7% in prior quarters — signaling a strategic shift toward preserving liquidity amid uncertain market conditions.
This asset-side expansion was fully matched on the liability side through corresponding growth in shares/deposits (+$326M or +1.2%), which provided stable, low-cost funding to support the increased cash buffer and loan portfolio without relying heavily on borrowed funds or volatile wholesale sources.

Total Liabilities totaled $8,505,210 thousand (in thousands), representing an 81.1% share of total assets and growing by $346 million quarter-over-quarter (+4.0%) to fund the expanded balance sheet; shares/deposits alone accounted for over 97% of this liability growth as members continued to deposit funds at a pace that outpaced withdrawals.
Accounts payable, accrued interest on borrowings, and other liabilities rose by $15M (23.9%), reflecting normal accruals tied to operational expenses and short-term financing costs rather than new debt issuance; meanwhile, allowance for credit losses remained flat at zero as no additional provisions were required during this reporting period.

Equity stood at $87,800 thousand (in thousands), with undivided earnings contributing $93.4 million to capital reserves and accumulated unrealized gains on available-for-sale securities showing a net loss of -$7.8 million due to market fluctuations in bond prices; equity acquired in merger added another $2.2M as part of ongoing consolidation activities.
Overall, the balance sheet reflects a conservative yet resilient posture: liquidity has been prioritized without sacrificing core lending capacity, funding remains stable and member-driven, and capital levels remain robust despite minor valuation adjustments on investment securities — positioning Campus Credit Union to navigate near-term volatility while maintaining long-term growth objectives.
